---
title: "DATRAS exchange tables"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(obus)
```

## Background

The object of this document is to map out the main exchange table structures and links. The structure is well known within the DATRAS community but was put forward here to aid thinking for a more novice person like me. It should be considered as an aid towards (re-)designing tables that makes downstream exploration and analysis as painless as possible. Given that a lot of thoughts is already embedded in already existing DATRAS products (e.g. CPUEL) the potential outcome may in the end only become some minor cosmetic changes.

AI was possibly used too profusely when generating this document and the current state has only been partially reviewed.

The four DATRAS exchange tables — **HH** (haul header), **HL** (catch at length), **CA** (catch at age), and **LT** (litter assessment) — are the authoritative source data submitted by national institutes. This article documents the variables in each table with three goals:

1. Identify what each variable means and where it lives
2. Flag redundancies that can be eliminated once tables are joined and values standardised
3. Propose a minimal, tidy representation of each table — including optimized parquet files suitable for downstream analysis

Old-style variable names are not used here except where the old/new naming difference is itself the point being discussed (see [Cross-table ambiguities]).

---

## The haul join key

Every child table (HL, CA, LT) begins with the same eight fields that together identify a unique haul. `dr_add_id()` collapses them into a single `.id` string:

```
Survey:Year:Quarter:Country:Platform:Gear:StationName:HaulNumber
```

| Variable | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Survey | chr | |
| Year | int | |
| Quarter | int | |
| Country | chr | ISO 3166 |
| Platform | chr | SeaDataNet code |
| Gear | chr | |
| StationName | chr | National coding, not ICES-standardised |
| HaulNumber | int | Sequential within cruise |

Once `.id` is present, **all eight fields are redundant in HL, CA, and LT**.

Three additional gear-description fields — `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, `DoorType` — are also repeated across HL, and CA for the same reason and are equally redundant in child tables.

If needed these variables, or for that matter any other variables in the HH table can always be "recalled" via simple join / lookup. Although the DATRAS data are not big trimming theses variables would reduce the clutter in the child tables, allowing for a more focused downstream analysis.

---

## HH — haul header

HH is the parent table: one row per haul. It carries everything about the fishing operation. The variables split naturally into those that form the join key (above) and those that are genuinely HH-only.

### Time

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| Month, Day | With Year (from join key) → `date` via `dr_add_date()` |
| StartTime | HHMM string → POSIXct via `dr_add_starttime()` |

Once `date` and `time` are derived, Month, Day, and StartTime become redundant.

### Haul outcome

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| HaulValidity | **V** = valid; the primary filter for all analyses |
| DataType | **R** raw, **S** bulk-subsampled, **C** pre-raised to 1 h, **P** size-stratified. Governs HL arithmetic. |
| HaulDuration | Minutes; needed to convert counts to `n_hour` |
| ReasonHaulDisruption | Why the haul was disrupted (if invalid) |

### Position and area

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| ShootLatitude, ShootLongitude | Primary spatial reference (start of tow) |
| HaulLatitude, HaulLongitude | End of tow |
| StatisticalRectangle | ICES rectangle; derivable from position |
| SurveyIndexArea | Survey-specific stratum used in index calculations |
| DepthStratum | Survey-specific depth band |
| DayNight | Derivable from position + time via NOAA solar calculator |

`StatisticalRectangle` and `DayNight` are derivable and are candidates for downstream derivation rather than storage. Same actually applies to `SurveyIndexArea` and `DepthStratum` if they can be derived from longitude and latitude possibly using appropriate spatial file. Because these positional areas may be frequently used in standardized analysis trimming may though not be an option.

### Gear configuration

`WarpLength`, `WarpDiameter`, `WarpDensity`, `DoorSpread`, `DoorSurface`, `DoorType`, `DoorWeight`, `WingSpread`, `NetOpening`, `SweepLength`, `GearExceptions`, `Rigging`, `CodendMesh`, `Buoyancy`, `GroundRopeWeight`, `KiteArea`, `Tickler`, `PelagicSamplingType`, `MinTrawlDepth`, `MaxTrawlDepth`.

Configuration and QC fields. For CPUE analyses the key derived quantities are swept area (from `DoorSpread` or `WingSpread` × `Distance`) and tow duration (`HaulDuration`). The raw fields are retained for swept-area calculations and gear-change diagnostics.

### Environmental observations

`BottomDepth`, `BottomTemperature`, `BottomSalinity`, `BottomCurrentDirection`, `BottomCurrentSpeed`, `SurfaceTemperature`, `SurfaceSalinity`, `SurfaceCurrentDirection`, `SurfaceCurrentSpeed`, `ThermoCline`, `ThermoClineDepth`, `SecchiDepth`, `Turbidity`, `WindDirection`, `WindSpeed`, `SwellDirection`, `SwellHeight`, `TidePhase`, `TideSpeed`, `HydrographicStationID`.

Ancillary environmental context. None are required for CPUE or index calculations.

### Administrative

`RecordHeader` (always "HH" — structural, drop), `EDMO` (originating institute), `BycatchSpeciesCode`, `StandardSpeciesCode`.

---

#### DataType lives in HH, not HL

`DataType` describes how the **entire haul's catch was processed** — it is a property of the fishing operation, not of any individual species record. A single haul has exactly one `DataType`; all HL rows for that haul share it. Storing it once in HH is the correct normalised design.

In practice, `DataType` (and `HaulDuration`) must be joined from HH before any HL arithmetic can be done correctly. The standard workflow is:

```r
hl |> left_join(hh |> select(.id, DataType, HaulDuration), by = ".id")
```

The same argument applies to CA: the sampling protocol is haul-level, not fish-level.

---

## HL — catch at length

HL is the most complex table because it conflates four distinct conceptual layers in a single flat structure.

### The four layers

**Layer A — haul join key** (redundant after `.id` join):
`Survey`, `Year`, `Quarter`, `Country`, `Platform`, `Gear`, `StationName`, `HaulNumber`. 

In addition we have `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, `DoorType` that are redundant because they are already in the haul table.

**Layer B — sampling protocol** (constant within a species × sex × category group???):

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `SpeciesValidity` | Record type — see below |
| `SpeciesCategory` | Subsampling category ID by size (up to 5 per species/sex/haul). Always 1 for DataType C. |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | Raise factor: 1/6 of catch measured → value = 6. Always 1 for DataType C. |
| `SubsampledNumber` | Count of fish actually measured |
| `SubsampleWeight` | Weight of the measured subsample (grams) |

**Layer C — catch totals** (values repeated identically across every length row of the group):

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `aphia` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesSex` | Population-level sex code for the group |
| `DevelopmentStage` | Non-invasive maturity/stage observation |
| `TotalNumber` | Total count per group — semantics depend on DataType, see below |
| `SpeciesCategoryWeight` | Total catch weight in grams per group |

**Layer D — length frequency** (one row per length bin):

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `LengthClass` | Lower boundary of bin (raw, unit set by `LengthCode`) |
| `LengthCode` | Unit flag → converts to `length_cm` |
| `LengthType` | Type of measurement (total length, fork length, etc.) |
| `NumberAtLength` | Count in subsample bin |

After standardisation, `LengthCode` + `LengthClass` → `length_cm`; the raw fields can be dropped.

### SpeciesValidity — the gatekeeper

`SpeciesValidity` controls what data are present in a row and whether it should be included in derived products:

| Value | Meaning | Use in products |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Invalid | No |
| 1 | Full: length frequencies, numbers, weights | Yes |
| 2 | Commercial species only; full data pending | Species-specific |
| 4 | Total number only; no lengths | Yes |
| 5 | Presence/absence only | Yes |
| 6 | Weight only | Yes |
| 7 | Numbers and weights; no lengths | Yes |
| 10 | Lengths and numbers; no weights | Yes |

Standard DATRAS products filter to `SpeciesValidity == 1`???. The obus functions expose this via the `specval` argument.

### Variables that depend on DataType

Three HL variables have semantics that shift with `DataType` (stored in HH, not in HL). This is the single most common source of silent errors when working with the exchange format.

**`TotalNumber`**

- DataType **R** or **S**: total number of specimens per haul for the species/sex/category group
- DataType **C**: total already raised to a 60-minute standard; `TotalNumber / 60 * HaulDuration` recovers the per-haul count

**`NumberAtLength`**

- DataType **R** or **S**: count of fish in the subsample length bin; multiply by `SubsamplingFactor` to estimate the haul-level count
- DataType **C**: the value is already a CPUE (numbers per hour) for that length bin; `NumberAtLength × SubsamplingFactor × HaulDuration / 60` recovers the per-haul count

**`SpeciesCategoryWeight`**

- DataType **R** or **S**: catch weight in grams per category group per haul
- DataType **C**: weight per hour (standardised to 60 minutes), analogous to `TotalNumber` for DataType C

In all three cases, the tidy parquets resolve the ambiguity: `n_haul` and `w_haul` in `HL_catch` are always per-haul counts/weights regardless of the original `DataType`.

### The redundancy problem

Layer C values (`TotalNumber`, `SpeciesCategoryWeight`) are **repeated identically across every length row of a group???**. A single species with 30 length bins produces 30 identical copies of the same total count and weight — ~97% redundancy for catch-level fields. This is the root cause of most HL confusion and the main motivation for the split described below.

---

## CA — catch at age

CA contains individual-level biological measurements on fish selected for ageing. Typically one row per measured fish; occasionally one row per length class when fish are not individually tracked.

### CA-specific variables (post-join)

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `aphia` | Species identity |
| `FishID` | Individual fish identifier within haul |
| `IndividualSex` | Sex by dissection — distinct from `SpeciesSex` in HL |
| `IndividualMaturity` | Maturity by dissection |
| `MaturityScale` | Scale used (M6 or SMSF from 2021+) |
| `LengthClass`, `LengthCode` | → `length_cm`; raw fields droppable |
| `LengthType` | Type of length measurement |
| `IndividualAge` | Age in years |
| `AgePlusGroup` | "+" flag when fish is assigned to the plus group |
| `AgeSource` | Tissue type (otolith, scale, etc.) |
| `AgePreparationMethod` | Preparation method |
| `OtolithGrading` | Otolith reader quality score |
| `IndividualWeight` | Grams |
| `LiverWeight` | Grams, when sampled |
| `NumberAtLength` | Fish count at this length/age class (when not individually measured) |
| `AreaCode`, `AreaType` | Sampling aggregation level — see cross-table ambiguity §5 |
| `StomachSamplingFlag` | Was stomach sampled? |
| `ParasiteSamplingFlag` | Was parasite sampling performed? |
| `GeneticSamplingFlag` | Was genetic sample taken? |

`RecordHeader`, `SweepLength`, `DoorType`, `GearExceptions` are all redundant with HH.
`SpeciesCode`, `SpeciesCodeType` are the raw submission fields, droppable once resolved to `aphia`.

---

## LT — litter assessment

LT records marine litter categories observed in trawl catches. All haul-level context is in HH; LT contributes only the litter observations themselves.

### LT-specific variables (post-join)

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `LTREF` | Reference list for litter categorisation |
| `PARAM` | Litter category code |
| `LTSZC` | Size class |
| `LTSRC` | Source of litter (if identifiable) |
| `LTPRP` | Litter properties (colour, etc.) |
| `TYPPL` | Plastic type |
| `LT_Items` | Count of items in category |
| `UnitItem` | Unit for count |
| `LT_Weight` | Weight |
| `UnitWeight` | Unit for weight |

Droppable after joining: `BottomDepth` (in HH), `OSPARArea`/`MSFDArea`/`EEZ`/`NMArea` (derivable from HH position via spatial join), `DateofCalculation` (admin), `Reserved1`/`Reserved2` (empty), `EDMO` (in HH), `RecordHeader` (structural).

---

## Cross-table ambiguities

### 1. Sex: `SpeciesSex` (HL) vs `IndividualSex` (CA)

Both tables have a variable with the same old-style name (`Sex`). The new-style names deliberately differ because the concepts differ:

- **`SpeciesSex`** (HL): population-level sex code for the catch group. Visual inspection of the bulk catch.
- **`IndividualSex`** (CA): sex determined by dissection of an individual fish.

These are not interchangeable. Joining HL and CA on sex without accounting for this distinction produces nonsense.

### 2. `NumberAtLength`: HL vs CA

| Table | Meaning |
|---|---|
| HL | Count in the subsample length bin. Multiply by `SubsamplingFactor` to estimate population count. |
| CA | Count of individually aged fish at that length class. Used as a raising key for age-length keys, not a population count. |

Same column name; completely different scaling logic.

### 3. `LengthClass`: bin boundary (HL) vs individual measurement (CA)

In HL, `LengthClass` is the *lower boundary* of a length frequency bin — many fish share the same value. In CA, it is the measured length of an individual fish (or its assigned bin for age-length key work). The values look alike but the interpretation is different.

### 4. Gear fields duplicated across HH, HL, CA

`Gear`, `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, and `DoorType` appear in all three tables with identical definitions. This is a submission artefact: each file must be self-describing. Analytically, HH is the sole source of truth. The copies in HL and CA should be dropped after joining.

### 5. `AreaCode` / `AreaType` in CA — per-area rows

CA rows are not always per-haul. When `AreaType != "H"` (Haul), age samples are pooled across multiple hauls within an area. In those cases the `.id` join to HH is ambiguous. This is a fundamental asymmetry with HL, which is always per-haul. When building age-length keys, check `AreaType` before assuming a 1:1 join with HH.

### 6. DataType-dependent variables in HL

`TotalNumber`, `NumberAtLength`, and `SpeciesCategoryWeight` all change meaning depending on `DataType`, which lives in HH not HL. This is covered in detail in the [HL section above](#variables-that-depend-on-datatype).

### 7. `aphia` naming across contexts

The resolved species AphiaID column has three names depending on context:

| Context | Name |
|---|---|
| Raw HL/CA parquets (ICES Datacenter) | `Valid_Aphia` |
| `dr_cpue_by_*()` output | `ValidAphiaID` |
| obus internal standard | `aphia` |

Until the canonical name is confirmed with the ICES Datacenter, be explicit about which source you are working with.

### 8. `RecordHeader` naming inconsistency

The old-style name is `RecordType` in CA but `RecordHeader` in FL. Both map to new-style `RecordHeader`. Relevant only when working directly with old-style submission files.

---

## Summary: drop or derive per table

| Variable(s) | Table(s) | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Platform, Gear, StationName, HaulNumber | HL, CA, LT | Drop after `dr_add_id()` + join to HH |
| GearExceptions, SweepLength, DoorType | HL, CA | Drop (duplicate of HH) |
| RecordHeader | all | Drop (always the table name) |
| Month, Day, StartTime | HH | → `date` + `time`; raw fields droppable |
| LengthCode + LengthClass | HL, CA | → `length_cm`; raw fields droppable |
| SpeciesCode + SpeciesCodeType | HL, CA | → `aphia`; raw fields droppable |
| StatisticalRectangle, DayNight | HH | Derivable from position + time |
| OSPARArea, MSFDArea, EEZ, NMArea | LT | Derivable from HH position |
| BottomDepth, EDMO | LT | Duplicate of HH |
| Reserved1, Reserved2, DateofCalculation | LT | Drop (empty / admin) |
| TotalNumber, SpeciesCategoryWeight | HL | → `n_haul`, `w_haul` (DataType-corrected) |

---

## Optimised parquet design

A recurring problem in trawl survey analysis is that every research group maintains its own translation script that converts the exchange format into something workable — handling `DataType`, length units, species codes, and subsampling arithmetic in subtly different ways. The results are not directly comparable even when the underlying data are identical. The obus parquets aim to eliminate this by providing **one authoritative set of analysis-ready tables** so that translation happens once, in one place, with documented choices.

The tables below describe that target design. The raw exchange parquets remain available (via `dr_con(...)`  with `raw = TRUE`) for users who need full fidelity to the submission format.

### `HH.parquet` (tidy)

One row per haul. `.id` is the join key for all child tables; the eight constituent fields are retained for filtering and grouping.

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | 8-field haul key |
| `Survey`, `Year`, `Quarter`, `Country`, `Platform`, `Gear`, `StationName`, `HaulNumber` | Retained for filtering and grouping |
| `date` | ISO date (from `Year` + `Month` + `Day`) |
| `time` | POSIXct shoot time, UTC |
| `HaulValidity` | Primary validity filter |
| `DataType` | R / S / C / P |
| `HaulDuration` | Minutes |
| `ShootLatitude`, `ShootLongitude` | Tow start position |
| `HaulLatitude`, `HaulLongitude` | Tow end position |
| `BottomDepth` | Metres |
| `SurveyIndexArea` | Survey-specific stratum |
| `DepthStratum` | Survey-specific depth band |
| `Distance` | Metres (for swept-area calculations) |
| `DoorSpread`, `WingSpread` | Metres (for swept-area calculations) |
| `BottomTemperature`, `SurfaceTemperature` | °C |
| `BottomSalinity`, `SurfaceSalinity` | PSU |
| `BycatchSpeciesCode`, `StandardSpeciesCode` | Which species lists were recorded |

Dropped: `RecordHeader`, `Month`/`Day`/`StartTime` (absorbed into `date`/`time`), `StatisticalRectangle`/`DayNight` (derivable), all remaining gear configuration and environmental fields (available in the raw parquet).

### HL split into three parquets

The exchange HL table conflates sampling protocol, catch totals, and length frequencies into one flat structure. The tidy design separates them. All three join to HH on `.id`; `HL_catch` and `HL_length` join to each other on `.id` × `aphia` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage` × `SpeciesCategory`.

#### `HL_protocol.parquet`

One row per `.id`. Describes how the catch was processed — independent of species. This is currently buried implicitly in HH (`DataType`) but is worth making explicit at the haul level in its own table for clarity.

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `DataType` | R / S / C / P — governs arithmetic in `HL_catch` and `HL_length` |
| `HaulDuration` | Minutes — repeated from HH for convenience; needed for n_hour conversion |

*Note:* in practice `DataType` and `HaulDuration` may simply be carried in `HH.parquet` (they already are in the design above). A separate `HL_protocol` table is only warranted if additional haul-level sampling metadata are added in future (e.g. mesh size used for subsampling).

#### `HL_catch.parquet`

One row per `.id` × `aphia` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage` × `SpeciesCategory`. Catch totals and subsampling bookkeeping; no length information.

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `aphia` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesValidity` | Record type — controls which rows to include in products |
| `SpeciesSex` | Population-level sex (visual inspection of bulk catch) |
| `DevelopmentStage` | Non-invasive stage observation |
| `SpeciesCategory` | Subsampling category ID (up to 5 per species/sex/haul) |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | Raise factor (e.g. 6 if 1/6 of catch was measured) |
| `SubsampledNumber` | Fish actually measured in the subsample |
| `SubsampleWeight` | Weight of the measured subsample (grams) |
| `n_haul` | Total count per haul, DataType-corrected (replaces raw `TotalNumber`) |
| `w_haul` | Total catch weight per haul in grams (replaces raw `SpeciesCategoryWeight`) |

`n_haul` and `w_haul` replace the ambiguous `TotalNumber` and `SpeciesCategoryWeight` after applying the DataType correction. Both are `NA` when not recorded (governed by `SpeciesValidity`).

#### `HL_length.parquet`

One row per `.id` × `aphia` × `SpeciesSex` × `DevelopmentStage` × `SpeciesCategory` × `length_cm`. Length frequency distribution only.

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `aphia` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `SpeciesSex` | |
| `DevelopmentStage` | |
| `SpeciesCategory` | |
| `length_cm` | Standardised length (from `LengthCode` + `LengthClass`) |
| `LengthType` | Fork / total / mantle / etc. |
| `NumberAtLength` | Count in subsample bin |
| `SubsamplingFactor` | Repeated here to avoid a mandatory join for the most common scaling operation |

`n_haul` and `n_hour` are **not** pre-computed — they are downstream products of `NumberAtLength × SubsamplingFactor` (and `HaulDuration` / `DataType` from HH for `n_hour`), and the arithmetic is straightforward once those inputs are in place.

### `CA.parquet` (tidy)

One row per individual fish measurement (most rows) or per length-class group (pooled age samples).

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `aphia` | Resolved WoRMS AphiaID |
| `AreaCode`, `AreaType` | Aggregation level — check before joining to HH |
| `FishID` | Individual identifier |
| `length_cm` | Standardised from LengthCode + LengthClass |
| `LengthType` | |
| `IndividualAge` | Years |
| `AgePlusGroup` | "+" flag |
| `AgeSource` | Otolith, scale, etc. |
| `AgePreparationMethod` | |
| `OtolithGrading` | Quality score |
| `IndividualSex` | Dissection-based |
| `IndividualMaturity` | |
| `MaturityScale` | |
| `IndividualWeight` | Grams |
| `LiverWeight` | Grams |
| `NumberAtLength` | For pooled rows only |
| `StomachSamplingFlag`, `ParasiteSamplingFlag`, `GeneticSamplingFlag` | |

Dropped: `RecordHeader`, haul key fields, `GearExceptions`, `SweepLength`, `DoorType`, `SpeciesCode`, `SpeciesCodeType`.

### `LT.parquet` (tidy)

One row per litter category per haul.

| Variable | Notes |
|---|---|
| `.id` | Join key |
| `LTREF` | Reference list |
| `PARAM` | Litter category |
| `LTSZC` | Size class |
| `LTSRC` | Source |
| `LTPRP` | Properties |
| `TYPPL` | Plastic type |
| `LT_Items` | Count |
| `UnitItem` | Unit for count |
| `LT_Weight` | Weight |
| `UnitWeight` | Unit for weight |

All spatial and haul-level context obtained by joining to `HH.parquet` on `.id`.
